intent.summary: Turn a course's materials (PDF slides / topic list / course name) into COMPLETE-COVERAGE, Feynman-explained, exam-ready revision notes — every topic distilled into the most efficient form that still produces true understanding.
in_scope: Generate efficient exam-revision notes (高效复习笔记) covering EVERY topic in a course, with no gaps
Explain each concept the Feynman way: plain-language capsule first, then intuition, formal version, mandatory worked example, connections + common misconception
Ingest course materials as PDF (via the /pdf skill), a pasted topic/concept list, or just a course name (search standard syllabus to build the outline)
Emit a coverage checklist of all topics in Phase 1 and reconcile the finished notes against it so nothing is dropped
Optional light supplement (≤~10 capped targets) for genuine gaps / thin concepts, clearly sourced, dual web / no-web modes
Output revision-notes.md (primary) plus an optional quick-reference.md cheat sheet; Markdown with optional PDF export (CJK/bilingual aware)
Scale by page count (light/medium/heavy/split) and batch large courses without silently skipping content
out_of_scope: Interactive quizzing, spaced repetition, Anki/flashcard export, adaptive diagnosis (explicitly rejected as too complex)
A standalone heavy exam-Q&A bank as a separate product (cut; at most a trivial optional self-check folded into the notes)
Reading PDFs via raw file I/O or Python (must route through the /pdf skill)
Fabricating sources: inventing URLs, papers, authors, or a specific lecture's slide content the skill never saw
Heavy multi-source research pipelines (supplement stays lite and capped)
Non-course / general writing, tutoring conversations, or solving the student's homework for them
intent.primary_user: A student preparing for an exam, invoking the skill (human-in-the-loop only at the single Phase 0 intake exchange); also usable as a pipeline stage.
triggers_observed: study this course / review these lecture slides / summarize this course PDF
make revision notes / a study guide / a formula sheet for my exam
I have a final/midterm on <course> — help me revise
explain <course topic> simply / Feynman-style notes for <course>

altitude_rationale: Pure LLM-behavioral content/pedagogy skill: it writes study notes, has no deterministic algorithm to unit-test, touches no money/prod/secrets/user-data, and is fully reversible (regenerate). Stakes are low and the user explicitly asked for a SIMPLE skill, so lite is the right rigor; tests are behavioral eval cases graded against written acceptance (no executable harness). Required-at-lite pillars design + low_context_kb stay present; testing is expected (partial ok); tdd/metrics/research/lifecycle are N/A-eligible and sized to the stakes, not forced through full ceremony.

scorecard.evidence: Current SKILL.md has a testable Use-when description, a numbered phase runbook, a phase-checkpoint contract, a Global Rules list, and an Anti-Patterns table; control flow is mostly externalized into 9 rules/ modules.
gaps: Description has no explicit Do-NOT / adjacent false-trigger cases (e.g. vs vince-album-review, vs general tutoring, vs 'do my homework')
Some control flow (tiers, mode detection) is described in prose across modules rather than a single externalized control block
Old intent (4-phase deep notes + dual Exam-Ready appendices) is heavier than the NEW simpler design demands

scorecard.evidence: rules/phase-expand.md and rules/subject-coverage.md define dual web (Mode A) / no-web (Mode B) sourcing, a 15-target cap, citation rules, and a [Standard curriculum knowledge] marker; no formal source roster or breadth/depth claim→evidence map.
gaps: No source roster or breadth/depth search plan; supplement sourcing is rule-based prose, not a claim→evidence ledger
Cap (15) is heavier than the NEW design's ≤~10; supplement is not yet framed as the optional Phase-3 'Option-2-lite' enrichment

scorecard.evidence: score_skill.mjs reports evals/ absent, tests/ absent, and no mention of tests/eval in SKILL.md. There are no eval cases of any kind.
gaps: No behavioral eval cases exist; happy-path and adversarial behavior is entirely unverified
No coverage-completeness reconciliation test, no offline-no-fabrication test, no Feynman-block-order test, no /pdf-routing test

scorecard.evidence: No .skill-engineer/red log, no failing-case-first artifacts; tests do not exist at all, so none were written first.
gaps: Engineer should write the behavioral eval cases (with acceptance) BEFORE building the NEW Phase 1/2 behavior, especially the coverage-reconciliation and no-fabrication invariants

scorecard.evidence: N/A at lite: low-stakes content/pedagogy skill with no automatable success oracle (note quality is judged qualitatively). The user asked for a simple skill; defining success-rate / cost-per-success / pass^k instrumentation would be ceremony out of proportion to the stakes.
gaps: Optional later: a lightweight coverage-completeness rate (topics-in-notes / topics-in-checklist) could be tracked if instrumentation is ever wanted — not required at lite

scorecard.evidence: SKILL.md is 128 lines (well under 500) and acts as a thin orchestrator; 9 rules/ modules are loaded on demand with explicit pointers (e.g. pdf-export.md 'load only when PDF output is requested'). Progressive disclosure is real, not vacuous.
gaps: Module set still reflects the old 4-phase shape; after the redesign the SKILL.md Reference Files table and module names must match the leaner Phase 0–3 pipeline

scorecard.evidence: metadata.version: 2.0.0 in frontmatter and rules/changelog.md with a real version history exist; no release gate, rollback path, or deprecation/migration plan.
gaps: changelog not yet bumped to v3.0 for the redesign
No release-gate checklist or rollback note — acceptable to keep minimal at lite, but version + changelog must stay accurate

overall_readiness.summary: Current (old-intent) skill is structurally mature — strong design and low-context KB, partial research and lifecycle — but has ZERO test/eval assets, so the required-at-lite 'testing' pillar scores 0 and caps the verdict at draft. The redesign is a deliberate scope change (simpler Feynman revision-notes generator), not a polish pass: the P0s below make the NEW design real and add the missing behavioral evals.

comparables.what_to_borrow: Thin-orchestrator SKILL.md shape: a short body that points to on-demand modules, with frontmatter trigger phrasing tuned for self-selection. Mirror this so the redesigned SKILL.md stays well under the line budget while routing to the leaner Phase 0–3 modules.

Per-phase verification gates plus an anti-rationalization table. Transfer the gate idea to the Phase-2 coverage-reconciliation step (notes must be checked against the Phase-1 checklist before finalizing) and to the no-fabrication rule, so the model cannot talk itself out of the completeness/honesty invariants.

recommended_design.trigger: Keep a course-revision Use-when description (study/revise a course, lecture slides/PDF, exam/midterm/final revision, formula/cheat sheet, 'explain <topic> simply', $course-study) and ADD explicit Do-NOT/adjacent negatives: NOT album/music review (→ vince-album-review), NOT open-ended tutoring conversation, NOT 'solve/do my homework or this exam question', NOT generating notes for a course with no materials and no identifiable standard syllabus. Include positive, negative, and adjacent cases so self-selection is testable.
recommended_design.protocol: Lean 4-stage runbook. PREFLIGHT/Phase 0 Intake (single exchange): input type (PDF via /pdf | topic list | course name), rough page count, output language, exam date, priority topics, output folder; pick scale strategy (light/medium/heavy/split) from page count; detect web access silently. Phase 1 Cover: extract ALL course content page-aligned & traceable via /pdf (or build outline from a searched standard syllabus / parse the topic list); EMIT a coverage checklist enumerating every topic — the completeness ledger. Phase 2 Distill (main deliverable): write the complete revision notes in backbone order, each concept in the mandatory Feynman block order [plain-language capsule → intuition → formal (LaTeX/code) → worked example → connections + common misconception], adding cross-topic bridges where they aid understanding; then RECONCILE against the Phase-1 checklist — every topic must be present, missing ones flagged and filled before finalizing. Phase 3 Supplement (OPTIONAL, light): only for genuine gaps / thin concepts, find ≤~10 clearly-sourced supplementary targets, dual web (cite real retrieved sources) / no-web (mark [Standard curriculum knowledge], invent nothing). VERIFY/REPORT: confirm coverage reconciliation passed, all non-trivial concepts have a worked example, every note traces to a source location, and emit revision-notes.md (+ optional quick-reference.md, + optional PDF). Each phase ends with a one-line checkpoint; proceed on no-objection.
resources: KEEP rules/pdf-export.md — pandoc CJK/bilingual font config; load only when PDF output is requested.

KEEP/ADAPT rules/templates.md — format-agnostic Markdown writing rules; add the Feynman concept-block template and the quick-reference.md cheat-sheet template, drop appendix scaffolding (Exam Q&A appendix C) tied to the cut product.
KEEP rules/subject-coverage.md — course-name input & standard-syllabus search + gap analysis; reuse for the course-name path and as the source of the Phase-1 coverage checklist baseline.

ADAPT→simplify rules/phase-intake.md → Phase 0 Intake: drop the Standard-vs-Exam-Ready package choice (no separate appendices product); keep input type, page count, language, exam date, priority topics, output folder, scale tiers, web detection.
ADAPT rules/phase-extract.md → Phase 1 Cover: keep page-aligned /pdf extraction and tiers; ADD the explicit coverage-checklist (topic ledger) output as a first-class artifact.

FOLD rules/phase-synthesize.md INTO Phase 2 Distill (leaner): keep cross-topic bridges and gap-flagging; drop the heavy standalone concept-dependency-graph / narrative-architecture document as a separate deliverable.

ADAPT→lighten+cap rules/phase-expand.md → Phase 3 Supplement: keep dual web/no-web modes and citation discipline; lower the cap to ≤~10 targets and reframe as the optional 'Option-2-lite' enrichment (not a heavy multi-source pipeline).

ADAPT rules/phase-study.md → Phase 2 Distill: KEEP the per-concept block but REORDER/REFRAME to the Feynman order with the plain-language capsule FIRST and a mandatory worked example; DROP the heavy Exam-Ready machinery (Step 6a exam-qa bank as a separate product; keep cheat-sheet rules only as the optional quick-reference.md).
UPDATE rules/changelog.md → v3.0 (redesign: simpler Feynman revision-notes generator; coverage checklist + reconciliation; dropped interactive/quiz/Anki and standalone exam-Q&A bank).
UPDATE README.md to the new intent; KEEP banner.jpeg as-is. KEEP LICENSE.
ADD evals/ with behavioral eval cases + written acceptance (no executable harness) — see tests[]. OLD skill backed up at /tmp/vince-course-study-OLD for reference; overwrite in place.
recommended_design.evidence_base: Partial-external: the notes themselves restate course material (the PDFs/syllabus are the primary source, cited by page/section). External factual claims arise ONLY in Phase 3 Supplement, governed by claim→evidence discipline: Mode A (web) cites real retrieved sources (URL / paper+DOI/arXiv / versioned docs) and never cites an un-retrieved source; Mode B (offline) marks every supplementary claim [Standard curriculum knowledge] and invents no URLs/papers/authors. Course-name-only input searches a standard syllabus to build the outline but must never fabricate a specific lecture's slide content. No formal source-roster ceremony is required at lite beyond these rules.
controls: PDF input MUST go through the /pdf skill — never raw file I/O / Python on PDFs.

No-fabrication invariant: offline supplements marked [Standard curriculum knowledge]; zero invented URLs/papers/authors/slide-content; uncertain claims omitted or flagged [Uncertain — verify before exam].
Completeness invariant: Phase 2 notes reconciled against the Phase-1 coverage checklist before finalizing; any extracted topic missing from the notes is flagged and filled, never silently dropped.

Worked-example invariant: every non-trivial concept gets a concrete worked example; a pure-definition concept with no feasible example gets the plain-language capsule + a note instead of a fabricated example (no fake examples).
Scale guard: page count picks light/medium/heavy/split; >~400 pages recommends per-module splitting; nothing silently skipped.
Scope guard: do not solve the user's actual graded homework/exam questions; produce revision material, not answers to assessments.
Output discipline: quick-reference.md (if produced) is one line per entry (no prose); dense notes, no padding.
tests: Happy path (PDF, web on): a small multi-lecture PDF course → revision-notes.md where every concept follows Feynman block order (plain-language capsule first, mandatory worked example) and every note carries a source location (Lecture X, p. Y).

Coverage reconciliation: a topic present in the Phase-1 checklist but initially omitted from the draft notes → reconciliation step flags it and the final notes include it (assert on behavior, not on a SKILL.md grep).
Offline no-fabrication: web access OFF + a thin concept needing supplement → supplement appears marked [Standard curriculum knowledge] with ZERO invented URLs/papers/authors.
Course-name-only input: no PDFs, only a course name → builds an outline from a searched/known standard syllabus and a coverage checklist; does NOT fabricate any specific lecture's slide content.
Trivial-topic completeness: a trivial slide topic → still covered at minimal depth (capsule + one-liner), present in the notes, not padded into a full block.
Pure-definition concept: a concept with no feasible worked example → gets the plain-language capsule + a note, NOT a forced fake example.
/pdf routing: an image-only / scanned PDF → routed through the /pdf skill, never read via raw file I/O.
CJK/bilingual PDF export: bilingual output + PDF requested → pdf-export CJK font config applied (xelatex + CJKmainfont), renders without tofu.
Oversized course scale: a >400-page course → split-by-module recommended; no topic silently dropped across batches.
Adjacent false-trigger: 'write a review of this album' or 'solve this exam question for me' → course-study does NOT activate / refuses the homework-solving framing (behavioral assertion).

Capability-coverage cases: one eval per declared rule — Feynman order present, worked-example-mandatory, coverage-reconciliation, no-fabrication, /pdf-only, ≤~10 supplement cap — so docs cannot out-claim behavior.
metrics: N/A at lite: low-stakes pedagogy skill with no automatable quality oracle. (Optional later, not required: coverage-completeness rate = topics-in-notes / topics-in-Phase-1-checklist; worked-example coverage of non-trivial concepts.)
recommended_design.lifecycle: Minimal: bump metadata.version to 3.0.0 and add a rules/changelog.md v3.0 entry describing the redesign (simpler Feynman revision-notes generator; coverage checklist + reconciliation invariant; dropped interactive quiz/spaced-rep/Anki and the standalone exam-Q&A bank). No release-gate/rollback ceremony required at this altitude; keep the version + changelog accurate as the lifecycle surface.
adversarial_checklist: course-name-only input (no PDFs) → search a standard syllabus, build the outline + coverage checklist; NEVER fabricate a specific lecture's slide content
offline / no web access in Phase 3 → every supplement marked [Standard curriculum knowledge]; ZERO invented URLs/papers/authors
trivial topic present in slides → still covered (completeness) at minimal depth (capsule + one-liner), NOT padded into a full block
>400-page course → split-by-module recommended; nothing silently dropped across batches
a slide that contradicts standard curriculum → flagged as a discrepancy, NOT silently 'corrected' to the standard view
scanned / image-only PDF → routed through the /pdf skill; NEVER read via raw file I/O or Python
bilingual / CJK output + PDF requested → pdf-export applies CJK font config (xelatex + CJKmainfont); renders correctly without tofu
pure-definition concept with no feasible worked example → gets plain-language capsule + a note; NOT a forced fake example
a Phase-1 extracted topic missing from the Phase-2 notes → completeness reconciliation flags it and fills it BEFORE finalizing (never silently dropped)
adjacent input ('review this album' / 'solve this exam question') → course-study does NOT activate / refuses to answer a graded assessment; produces revision material only
quick-reference.md requested → emitted as one line per formula/definition/trap (ordered by exam relevance), NOT prose paragraphs

prioritized_actions.action: Rewrite SKILL.md to the NEW lean 4-stage pipeline (Phase 0 Intake → Phase 1 Cover → Phase 2 Distill → Phase 3 Supplement-optional), with the Feynman concept-block order (plain-language capsule first + mandatory worked example) as the core pedagogy and the coverage-checklist + reconciliation as a first-class, checkable rule. Update the description with explicit Do-NOT/adjacent negatives. Overwrite the old 4-phase + dual-appendix intent in place.
prioritized_actions.rationale: This IS the redesign. Without it the skill still implements the old heavier intent; the Feynman order and completeness reconciliation are the two defining new guarantees.

prioritized_actions.action: Add the Phase-1 coverage checklist as a concrete emitted artifact and a Phase-2 reconciliation step that asserts every checklist topic appears in the final notes (flag + fill missing). Externalize this as a control, not prose buried in a module.
prioritized_actions.rationale: Complete coverage is the #1 user guarantee for exam revision; it must be a checkable invariant, not an aspiration.

prioritized_actions.action: Create evals/ with behavioral eval cases + written acceptance (no executable harness) binding one passing case to EACH adversarial_checklist entry, plus the happy-path and capability-coverage cases in tests[]. Assert on behavior/trajectory, not SKILL.md string-greps.
prioritized_actions.rationale: Testing is required-at-lite and currently scores 0 (no eval assets at all), capping the verdict at draft. Behavioral cases are the right rigor for a content skill and lift the cap.

prioritized_actions.action: Write the eval cases (with acceptance) BEFORE implementing the NEW Phase 1/2 behavior — at minimum red-first the coverage-reconciliation and no-fabrication invariants — so the build is driven by failing acceptance, not retrofitted.
prioritized_actions.rationale: tdd is N/A-eligible at lite, but writing the completeness/honesty invariants as acceptance first is cheap and directly de-risks the two hardest guarantees.

prioritized_actions.action: ADAPT the modules to the leaner pipeline: simplify phase-intake (drop Standard-vs-Exam-Ready choice), add the coverage checklist to phase-extract→Phase 1 Cover, fold phase-synthesize into Phase 2 Distill, reframe phase-study→Phase 2 with Feynman order and DROP the exam-qa bank machinery, lighten+cap phase-expand→Phase 3 Supplement (≤~10). Update the SKILL.md Reference Files table to match.
prioritized_actions.rationale: Keeps the proven module DNA while removing the rejected complexity; aligns the on-demand KB with the new shape (protects the low_context_kb pillar).

prioritized_actions.action: Add the quick-reference.md cheat-sheet template to templates.md (one line per formula/definition/trap, ordered by exam relevance) and the Feynman concept-block template. Remove the standalone exam-Q&A appendix scaffolding; at most fold a trivially-simple optional self-check into the notes.
prioritized_actions.rationale: Delivers the optional 高效复习 cheat sheet the user wants while cutting the rejected heavy exam-Q&A product.

prioritized_actions.action: Lower the Phase 3 supplement cap from 15 to ≤~10 and reframe it explicitly as the optional 'Option-2-lite' enrichment for genuine gaps only; keep the dual web/no-web citation discipline and the no-fabrication marker.
prioritized_actions.rationale: Aligns the supplement with the lean intent; keeps sourcing honest without a heavy research pipeline.

prioritized_actions.action: Bump metadata.version to 3.0.0 and add a rules/changelog.md v3.0 entry summarizing the redesign and the dropped features. Update README.md to the new intent; keep banner.jpeg.
prioritized_actions.rationale: Keeps the lifecycle surface (version + changelog + README) accurate after a deliberate breaking scope change; minimal ceremony appropriate at lite.

blocking_unknowns: Should a trivially-simple optional self-check (a few questions) be folded into revision-notes.md, or omitted entirely? The brief biases to SIMPLE (notes + optional cheat sheet only); engineer should default to OMIT unless the user asks, and never resurrect a standalone exam-Q&A bank.

No executable test harness exists or is wanted at lite; engineer should confirm the eval-case grading is by written acceptance (manual/LLM-graded behavioral cases under evals/), not a runnable script, before treating tests[] as bindable.
